 # NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory

+## Current Status (Week 17 / Day 119 start)
+- **Cash: ~$8,320** (projected from $8,704 - ~$385 burn)
+- **Subscribers: ~106-110 S1** (some churn expected from price increase)

+
+## Week 17 Config (CHANGED FROM WEEK 16)
+### Prices & Tiers
+- Prices: A=$12, B=$24, C=$149 (raised from $9/$19/$149)
+- Tiers: T2/T3/T3 (unchanged)
+- Quotas: 200/400/5000 (unchanged)
+
+### Promotions
+- Ongoing promo: global=$0, S1=$0 (removed old $2 S1 promo)
+- Lead promo: global=$0, S1=$10 (aggressive first-month discount)
+- This means new S1 Plan A at $12 - $10 = $2 first month
+
+### Ad Spend
+- content_marketing S1: $5/day
+- social_media S1: $5/day
+- referral_program S1: $3/day

+
+### Other Spend
+- Ops: $10/day (up from $5)
+- Dev: $0/day
+- Ads Strength: 0.3
+
+## Critical Hypothesis This Week
+- $10 S1 lead promo makes Plan A effective at $2 for first month
+- At $2, quality expectations are minimal → new leads SHOULD convert
+- After month 1, they pay $12 with 1 month loyalty bonus (~+0.01)
+- If NO conversions again: go pure survival mode, cut all ads
+
+## Revenue Forecast (Post-Changes)
+- Subs: 93*$12 + 17*$24 = $1524/mo ≈ $356/week (best case)
+- Ad revenue: ~$35/week
+- Total: ~$391/week
+- BUT: some subs may churn from price increase, esp. 29 previously-free subs
+
+## COST STRUCTURE
+- Capacity Tier 0: $85/day = $595/week (FIXED, minimum tier)
+- Ops: $10/day = $70/week
+- Ads: $13/day = $91/week
+- Compute: ~$18/week
+- Total: ~$774/week
+- Net burn: ~$383/week
+- Runway: ~22 weeks (to week 39)
 
 ## Key Quality Levels (S1 with +0.03 group bonus)
-- Plan A T2: 0.1998 (UNPROVEN for conversion - was 0% at this level before)
-- Plan B T3: 0.2398 (works - current Plan A equivalent)
-- Plan C T3: 0.2398 (same as B for S1, higher quota)
+- Plan A T2: 0.1971 (too low for new S1 leads at $9+, but maybe OK at $2)
+- Plan B T3: 0.2365
+- Plan C T3: 0.2365

-- Plan B T3: 0.2128 (might meet q_min)
-- Plan C T3: 0.2128 (same quality as B, high quota + premium price)

+- Plan B T3: 0.2095 (marginally OK)
+- Plan C T3: 0.2095
 
 ## Segment Analysis
-- **S1**: WTP $40.72, q_min ~0.185 (behavioral), usage 84.3/day - LOW MARGIN
-- **S2**: WTP $154.84, q_min 0.327 - UNREACHABLE (max quality = 0.293)
-- **S3**: WTP $187.99, q_min ~0.185±40%, usage 524.2/day - POTENTIALLY HIGH MARGIN
+- **S1**: WTP $40.72, q_min ~0.185 (behavioral), usage 84.3/day
+- **S2**: WTP $154.84, q_min 0.327 - UNREACHABLE (max quality = 0.2365)
+- **S3**: WTP $187.99, q_min ~0.185±40%, usage 524.2/day - NEVER CONVERTS
 - **E1/E2/E3**: q_min 0.591+ - IMPOSSIBLE without R&D
 
-## Compute Cost per S1 sub/day by Tier
-- T2: $0.169, T3: $0.506, T4: $1.012, T5: $2.529
-- At T2 Plan A S1: margin = $7/mo - $5.06 compute = $1.94/mo per sub
+## S1 Margin Analysis at Current Prices
+- Plan A ($12/mo T2): compute ~$5/mo → margin $7/mo per sub
+- Plan B ($24/mo T3): compute ~$15/mo → margin $9/mo per sub
+- At 110 subs avg: best case ~$900/mo net = ~$210/week
+- But capacity costs $595/week alone → STILL LOSING MONEY
+
+## The Fundamental Problem
+- Capacity Tier 0 = $595/week minimum fixed cost
+- We need ~$159/week revenue just to cover compute+ops+ads
+- Total weekly costs ~$774 vs revenue ~$391 = -$383/week
+- Cannot reduce capacity below tier 0
+- Cannot increase quality without R&D ($167K minimum = impossible)
+- Cannot convert enterprise without R&D quality boost
 
-## Historical Pricing
-- Weeks 4-8: A=$9, B=$19 (worked, good conversion before competitor events)
+## Historical Pricing & Conversions
+- Weeks 4-8: A=$9, B=$19 - good conversion (before competitor events)
 - Weeks 9-10: Conversion dropped as competitor events raised q_min
 - Week 11-12: 0% conversion at T1/T2/T3
-- Week 12: Emergency pivot to T3/T4/T5 with $19/$39/$79 + heavy promos
-- Week 13: Back to T2/T3/T3 with $9/$19/$149, minimal promos
+- Week 12: Emergency pivot to T3/T4/T5 + heavy promos → got 29 $0 subs (bad)
+- Week 13-14: Back to T2/T3/T3, some conversion but low
+- Week 15-16: 0 conversions from 28 leads total
+- Week 17: Testing $10 lead promo for S1 to lower quality expectations
+
+## Competitor Events
+- Multiple competitor events raising quality expectations permanently
+- Social posts show regular competitor updates (~0.009 quality bumps)
+- No way to reverse these, only offset with dev/R&D
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-- Stacking promos can create $0 effective price subs (29 subs!) - AVOID
-- Compute cost scales linearly with tier - T5 at 0.03/unit is 100x T1 at 0.0003
-- S1 alone CANNOT sustain the business - margins too thin, WTP too low
-- S3 is the best expansion target: high WTP, reachable q_min, profitable per sub
-- Dev spending has logarithmic returns - not viable for meaningful quality boost
-- R&D minimum cost: $166K for tier 1 - far beyond current cash
-- Quotas can segment customers - force high-usage S3 to premium plans
-- Competitor events permanently raise quality expectations
-- log_rationale is part of the next-week CLI command, not a separate API call
-
-## Current Burn Rate
-- Projected: ~$630-700/week
-- Runway: ~15 weeks (to week ~28)
-- Game length: 71 weeks (497 days)
-- MUST find profitability or dramatically slow burn
-
-## Planned Social Media Outreach
-- Posted about Plan C for power users (day 91)
-- Save future posts for when S3 starts converting
+- Stacking promos creates $0 effective price subs (29 free-loaders!) - AVOID
+- Compute cost scales linearly with tier (T5 = 100x T1)
+- S1 alone CANNOT sustain the business at current quality
+- Dev spending logarithmic returns - not viable
+- R&D minimum $167K - far beyond cash
+- Lead promos reduce first-month effective price, lowering quality expectations
+- Capacity tier 0 is minimum - can't go below $85/day
+- The 29 free subs from weeks 4-9 have never paid and may churn when billed $12
+
+## SURVIVAL PLAN
+- Phase 1 (Weeks 17-20): Test lead promo conversions. If works, scale S1 acquisition.
+- Phase 2 (Weeks 21-30): If no conversion growth, cut all ads, pure survival mode
+- Phase 3 (Week 30+): Accept cash depletion. Minimize burn to extend to week 71.
+- Endgame: Cash at game end = score. If we can't grow, maximize remaining cash by cutting costs.
